name: Uusimaa (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the historic Uusimaa arms, evoking the Gulf of Finland and southern coastal waters
the silver charges of the arms

description: A square blue flag bearing six silver heraldic figures arranged in a 2x3 grid, the heraldic banner of the Uusimaa coat of arms.
history: Uusimaa became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic arms of the Uusimaa province on Finland's southern coast.

features.symbolism: six silver wave-like figures arranged in a 2x3 grid on the blue field, derived from the historic provincial arms of Uusimaa and referring to the maritime character of Finland's southern coast.
